what_it_does: This file is the runnable entrypoint for the shared-channel boundary. It loads a least-privilege env file (with existing os.environ taking precedence), builds a Supabase principal, opens an outbound Realtime subscription to Supabase channel_posts, and injects each new shared-channel post into live company member sessions via cc_channels.push. It also provides a publish hook (post_to_channel) that routes shared posts outward to Supabase while leaving internal posts on the existing local fabric, seeds a shared 'design' channel, starts the boundary as a daemon, and offers a live end-to-end self-test (verify_my_half).
observations: Env loading defaults to build-prep/claude-design/supabase/.boundary.env and is overridable via COMPANY_CHANNEL_ENV_FILE; existing non-empty os.environ values always win.
Canonical env keys use the COMPANY_CHANNEL_ prefix: SA_EMAIL, SA_PASSWORD, ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_URL.
The Realtime topic is the module constant _REALTIME_TOPIC = 'public.channel_posts'.
make_inject and make_members_of are closure factories that import runtime.cc_channels at call time.
post_to_channel is fail-closed: cc_channels.is_shared(channel) must be true to publish outward; otherwise it falls back to the existing internal local broadcast via cc_channels.send.
ensure_design_channel idempotently creates/updates a shared 'design' channel and adds supplied member handles.
run_boundary loads env, builds the subscriber/principal, starts the Realtime thread, and optionally blocks forever.
verify_my_half performs auth -> WS join -> publish -> receive on the 'design' channel using a no-op inject and a marker string to prove RLS streaming.
The __main__ block dispatches to verify_my_half when the first argument is 'verify', otherwise runs the boundary daemon.
The file repeatedly states it only reads the internal coordination fabric and does not alter send/push/route.
standouts: The live 'my-half' round-trip self-test (auth -> join -> publish -> receive) with a no-op inject and marker detection.
The dual-path publish hook that explicitly keeps internal posts inside the box while sending shared posts to Supabase.
The env-loading precedence rule designed so local reads a file and prod sets vars directly without clobbering.
conventions: Heavy module and function docstrings explaining design intent and constraints.
Closure factories for inject and members_of to defer imports.
Fail-closed routing: shared=False defaults to internal local broadcast.
Broad exception catching around per-member delivery so one dead session does not block others.
concerns: verify_my_half monkey-patches sub.on_insert and uses time.sleep polling loops rather than event-driven synchronization.
post_to_channel catches broad Exception on internal sends, which could mask real bugs.
load_env_file strips surrounding quotes but does not handle inline comments, escaped quotes, or values containing '='.
verify_my_half leaves a SELFTEST row in the shared 'design' channel and uses a hardcoded from_session value 'ch-8djrpmsl'.
There is no visible token refresh or expiry handling for the long-running subscriber.
notes: Boundary service entrypoint that wires external Supabase shared-channel traffic into local sessions while explicitly leaving the internal coordination fabric untouched.
questions: What is the exact contract and lifecycle of ChannelInjectSubscriber (start/stop/on_insert/on_status)?
How does runtime.cc_channels.push differ from send, and what does is_shared evaluate?
What RLS policy on channel_posts guarantees the principal only receives rows it should stream?
How is the Supabase access token refreshed during a long-running boundary process?
What does build_post_row and publish_shared_post do, and what fields are required by the Supabase table?
How are dead sessions detected and reported by cc_channels.push?
What is the intended semantics of thread and to_session for shared posts?
What does 'MODEL-1 single-source' refer to in this codebase?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it loads env/credentials, builds an outbound Realtime subscriber, injects shared posts into local sessions, provides a shared/internal publish hook, seeds the design channel, and verifies the pipe by live use.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for shared channels (Supabase)
Live verification rather than offline green checks
Do not alter existing internal fabric
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a safe, read-only-from-internal-fabric bridge that lets external Supabase shared-channel posts reach existing company member sessions, while keeping internal coordination unchanged and providing a concrete live test that the auth->publish->Realtime->receive path works.
novelty: The explicit 'my-half' live round-trip verification that uses a no-op inject and a marker row to prove RLS streaming without touching real sessions, and the strong architectural stance that the boundary is additive and only reads the internal fabric.
contribution: This file contributes the runnable boundary entrypoint, env-loading precedence logic, the publish hook that routes shared posts outward and internal posts locally, the shared 'design' channel seeding helper, and a live self-test that exercises the auth->Realtime->receive path.
summary_for_embedding: Runnable boundary entrypoint that loads Supabase credentials, subscribes to Realtime channel_posts, injects shared-channel inserts into local company member sessions via cc_channels.push, routes outbound shared posts through Supabase while keeping internal posts local, seeds a shared 'design' channel, and includes a live end-to-end self-test.
intention_for_embedding: Provide an additive, least-privilege bridge between external Supabase shared channels and the existing internal session fabric without modifying internal send/route logic.

symbols.does: Loads KEY=VALUE env file into os.environ with existing os.environ taking precedence.
symbols.description: Reads the boundary env file (default repo-relative path or COMPANY_CHANNEL_ENV_FILE override), skips missing files, ignores comments/blank lines, and sets only keys not already present and non-empty in os.environ. Returns the list of keys set, never their values.

symbols.does: Formats a channel_posts row into (content, meta) for cc_channels.push.
symbols.description: Extracts the row's text as content and builds a meta dict with from, thread, and channel attributes so the injected message appears to local sessions as a real channel post from its original author.

symbols.does: Factory returning an inject closure that delivers shared-channel rows into live sessions.
symbols.description: Imports runtime.cc_channels and returns a function that takes a member handle and a channel_posts row, formats it, and calls cc_channels.push to deliver the message without creating a local _mail.jsonl record.

symbols.does: Delivers a formatted shared-channel row to one member handle via cc_channels.push.
symbols.description: The closure produced by make_inject; it converts the row to content and meta, then pushes it to the supplied handle. Exceptions are intended to propagate to the subscriber's on_insert handler.

symbols.does: Factory returning a members_of closure for a channel id.
symbols.description: Imports runtime.cc_channels and returns a function that returns the list of member handles for a given channel_id, returning an empty list on any exception so absent channels are treated as having no members.

symbols.does: Returns the member handles of a channel, or [] on error.
symbols.description: The closure produced by make_members_of; it delegates to cc_channels.channel_members and silently swallows exceptions, treating unknown or absent channels as empty.

symbols.does: Constructs a ChannelInjectSubscriber with principal, inject, members_of, and the Realtime topic.
symbols.description: Creates a SupabasePrincipal with prefix COMPANY_CHANNEL, builds inject and members_of closures, and instantiates ChannelInjectSubscriber configured for the public.channel_posts topic. Does not start the subscriber.

symbols.does: Simple status logger printing [boundary] prefixed lines to stdout.
symbols.description: A small helper used as the default on_status callback; it prints kind and detail with flush for observable boundary events.

symbols.does: Publishes a post either outward to Supabase (shared) or internally to local members.
symbols.description: The shared-aware publish hook: if the channel is shared it builds a channel_posts row and publishes via publish_shared_post; otherwise it broadcasts locally via cc_channels.send to each channel member, catching per-member errors so one dead session does not block others.

symbols.does: Idempotently seeds a shared 'design' channel and adds members.
symbols.description: Reads/creates the 'design' channel, ensures its shared flag is true, writes it back if changed, and attempts to add each supplied member handle while ignoring membership-related failures.

symbols.does: Loads env, builds and starts the Realtime subscriber, optionally blocking forever.
symbols.description: Orchestrates the boundary service: calls load_env_file, builds the subscriber and principal, starts the subscription thread, logs startup, and if block=True keeps the process alive until KeyboardInterrupt stops the subscriber.

symbols.does: Performs a live auth->join->publish->receive round-trip on the 'design' channel.
symbols.description: The by-use verification: loads env, authenticates, starts a ChannelInjectSubscriber with no-op inject and a status watcher, publishes a SELFTEST marker row to the shared 'design' channel, and waits to see the same marker come back through Realtime to prove the RLS-gated path is live.

symbols.does: Status callback that records join success and collects notes.
symbols.description: Inner function used as the subscriber's on_status during verification; it sets 'joined' to True when kind is 'joined' and appends all status events to the output notes list.
Inner function assigned as the subscriber's inject during verify_my_half; it does nothing because the test only proves receive, avoiding any effect on live member sessions.

symbols.does: Wraps on_insert to flag when the published marker row is received.
symbols.description: Inner function monkey-patched onto sub.on_insert; it checks whether the row text contains the selftest marker and, if so, sets a seen flag, then delegates to the original on_insert handler.
